votes.desc: House Comm - Motion to Recommend Failed

calendar.description: Senate Transportation, Public Utilities, Energy, and Technology Hearing

general_provisions: This bill amends provisions related to transportation, including road usage charge, towing dispatch, and local option sales tax use.
hilighted_provisions: This bill:<hr><ltbullet>allows a political subdivision to prohibit a mobile business from operating on a street or sidewalk that is temporarily closed for certain events;<hr><ltbullet>requires commercial electric vehicles with a gross weight of 6,001 pounds or more to pay a road usage charge with a higher road usage charge cap;<hr><ltbullet>exempts buses operating on the bus's planned route to temporarily cross lane lines to stop at a bus stop;<hr><ltbullet>exempts the Department of Transportation from certain requirements related to receiving a conveyance of real property;<hr><ltbullet>provides a sales tax exemption for construction materials purchased by the Department of Transportation for certain public transit capital development projects;<hr><ltbullet>amends certain allowed uses of local option sales taxes for transportation;<hr><ltbullet>increases bonding authorization related to commuter rail expansion;<hr><ltbullet>defines the Utah trail network;<hr><ltbullet>delegates certain authority to the executive director of the Department of Transportation during a natural disaster;<hr><ltbullet>requires local governments that receive funds from the County of the First Class Highway Projects Fund to report on the expenditure of funds and the progress of projects;<hr><ltbullet>allows the Transportation Commission to prioritize the use of Transit Transportation Investment Fund revenue for corridor preservation purposes and provides parameters for the use of the revenue;<hr><ltbullet>allows certain rail road transportation funds to be used to repair tracks and rail infrastructure located on state lands;<hr><ltbullet>allows the Department of Transportation to waive sovereign immunity for public transit projects;<hr><ltbullet>amends the definition of "state transportation purposes";<hr><ltbullet>amends provisions related to the ability to charge towing dispatch service fees and pass through a reasonable towing dispatch service fee;<hr><ltbullet>repeals the study required by the Department of Transportation and Salt Lake City regarding highway reduction strategies;<hr><ltbullet>codifies certain requirements based on the findings and results of the study on highway reduction strategies; and<hr><ltbullet>makes technical and conforming changes.
